name: Cinematic Film-Style
description: Blockbuster, film-look short — 8-15s of dramatic cinema language: 2-second hook, deliberate camera language (dolly, crane, rack focus), motivated lighting (chiaroscuro / golden hour / volumetric), and synced sound design. Generic prompt cookbook for any subject; pick a hook, a lighting setup, a camera move, and a color grade, then run.

stackSummary.keyframes: google/gemini-3-pro-image-preview via OpenRouter — establish the cinematic frame (lighting, lens, palette, atmosphere) before motion. One keyframe per beat (hook / establish / climax / resolve).
stackSummary.i2v: kwaivgi/kling-v3.0-pro via OpenRouter for standard 5-8s clips. Switch to google/veo-3.1 for premium motion fidelity, or openai/sora-2-pro for sustained 10-15s shots when budget allows. generate_audio: false — audio mixed separately.
stackSummary.voiceover: ElevenLabs eleven_multilingual_v2 — sparse and weighted. One emotional line at the climax beats wall-to-wall narration. Add 0.3-1.2s reverb tail to suggest space.
stackSummary.captions: Optional. If used, MinimalCaptions only. HormoziCaptions break cinema language — never combine impact-pop with film-look.
stackSummary.music: ElevenLabs Music — orchestral / ambient / synth bed. Build through the clip, peak at the climax frame, resolve in the last 2s. Lock cuts to musical downbeats.

constants.motionDoctrine: every shot has one named camera move + one motivated light source

variationAxes: mood (noir / epic / intimate / dystopian / nostalgic / awe)
palette (teal-orange / desaturated-with-accent / golden-warm / cool-blue / bleach-bypass / monochrome)
lighting setup (chiaroscuro / three-point / silhouette / golden-hour / volumetric / practical-neon / firelight)
camera move (push-in / pull-back / crane / orbit / whip-pan / handheld / lock-off / rack-focus)
hook technique (black-to-burst / extreme-macro / reverse-motion / silent-then-explosive / eyes-open / dutch-angle)
duration (4s compact / 8s standard viral / 10s premium short / 15s full arc)
doNotCopyLiterally: Cinema language is generic. Do not copy a specific movie's iconic shot, character, or branded prop. Pick a hook + lighting + camera + grade combination and write the rest from the brief.
referenceNotes: Optional but useful: a movie still or a single screenshot pinning the desired palette and lighting (one image is enough). If the brief names a real person, brand, or product → AGENTS.md hard rule #3 still applies (reference required for that named entity). The cinematic style itself does not need a reference.
